core_principles: Fusion over Imitation: Academic Trust meets Cyberpunk Depth
Emotion First: Focus states should feel like 'locking in' to study mode
Content is King: Metadata (tags, subject) is the hero, not just the file name
Negative Space: Use 'Void' spacing to prevent cognitive overload
anti_patterns: No pure black (#000000) backgrounds - use Deep Navy/Charcoal
No generic 'Bootstrap' grids - use Bento layouts
No default scrollbars - use thin, custom-colored tracks

rules: Overlines/Labels: uppercase, tracking-widest (0.1em), text-xs, font-medium, text-muted-foreground

utility_classes.glass_input: bg-black/20 backdrop-blur-sm border-white/10 focus:border-primary/50 transition-colors duration-300

spacing_and_layout.grid_system: Bento Grid (mix of col-span-1, col-span-2, row-span-2)

buttons.primary: rounded-full bg-primary hover:bg-primary/90 text-white font-medium px-8 py-6 shadow-[0_0_20px_rgba(59,130,246,0.3)] transition-all hover:scale-105 active:scale-95
buttons.secondary: rounded-full bg-white/5 hover:bg-white/10 border border-white/10 text-white backdrop-blur-md px-6 py-6 transition-all

cards.document_card: group relative overflow-hidden rounded-2xl bg-[#12141F] border border-white/5 hover:border-primary/50 transition-all duration-300 hover:shadow-[0_0_30px_rgba(59,130,246,0.15)]
cards.upload_zone: border-2 border-dashed border-white/10 rounded-3xl bg-white/5 hover:bg-white/10 hover:border-primary/50 transition-all duration-300 flex flex-col items-center justify-center p-12 cursor-pointer

inputs.default: h-12 bg-black/20 border-white/10 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/20 focus:border-primary text-white placeholder:text-slate-600 rounded-xl

home.layout: Hero with Search + Bento Grid Categories + Featured Docs (Carousel)

document_detail.layout: Split View: Left (Preview/Thumbnail), Right (Metadata, Rating, Comments)

profile.layout: Header (Avatar + Stats) + Tabs (My Docs, Favorites, Settings)

universal_guidelines: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
